title: Today's AI Brief: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
summary: A brief look at AI agents trading stocks on Robinhood and South Africa's AI policy landscape.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
imageCaption: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
imageSourceTitle: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. We're seeing some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence, particularly in the areas of finance and policy. Let's dive in.

First up, Robinhood is now allowing AI agents to trade stocks on behalf of users. This move is part of a larger trend of companies enabling AI agents to make payments and trade on users' behalf. It's an interesting development, as it raises questions about the role of AI in financial decision-making. I mean, are we letting robots make our investment decisions? It's a bit like having a robot doing your taxes - convenient, but also a little unsettling.

Also making headlines is South Africa's draft AI policy. According to a report from Spectrum IEEE, the country's policy landscape is leaving its AI leverage unused. It's a story that highlights the challenges of governing AI in a developing country. The intersection of technology and policy is always fascinating, and South Africa's situation is a great example of how complex it can be.

And while we're on the topic of AI and finance, I came across another interesting piece of news. Unfortunately, I don't have the details here, but it was about a company using AI to detect financial anomalies. It's amazing to see how AI is being applied in so many different areas, from trading stocks to preventing financial crime.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
